name: Regulatory Document Builder
description: A tool for teams assembling regulatory filings (prospectuses, offering memoranda) to manage documents made up of reusable sections, each with its own approval workflow.

requirements.title: Project Management
requirements.description: Create and manage document projects (e.g. Q3 Prospectus Update) with metadata, deadline, assigned team. Full CRUD -- create projects, view them, edit details, archive when done.

stories.title: Manage document projects
stories.iWant: to create, view, edit, and archive document projects
stories.soThat: I can track and organise our regulatory filings

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am on the projects page
acceptanceCriteria.when: I click 'New Project' and fill in name, description, deadline, and team
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new project is created and appears in the project list

acceptanceCriteria.when: I click on it in the list
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see the full project detail view with all metadata

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am viewing a project detail
acceptanceCriteria.when: I click Edit and change fields then save
acceptanceCriteria.then: the project is updated and changes persist

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am viewing a project detail
acceptanceCriteria.then: the project is removed from the system

requirements.title: Section Library
requirements.description: Reusable section templates (risk factors, management discussion, financial summary, legal disclaimers). Sections written in markdown, displayed as formatted rich text. Adding a section to a project creates an editable working copy from the template. Full CRUD on templates and working copies.

stories.title: Manage section templates
stories.iWant: to create, view, edit, and delete reusable section templates
stories.soThat: I have a library of standard sections to draw from

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am on the section templates page
acceptanceCriteria.when: I click 'New Template' and fill in name, category, and markdown content
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new template is created and appears in the library
the markdown content is rendered as formatted rich text
it is removed from the library

stories.title: Add sections to projects
stories.iWant: to add a template to a project as an editable working copy
stories.soThat: I can customise section content for each specific filing

acceptanceCriteria.when: I click 'Add Section' and select a template
acceptanceCriteria.then: a working copy of that template is created within the project

acceptanceCriteria.given: a working copy exists in a project
acceptanceCriteria.when: I edit its content and save
acceptanceCriteria.then: the working copy is updated without affecting the original template

acceptanceCriteria.given: a working copy exists in a project
acceptanceCriteria.then: the markdown content renders as formatted rich text

requirements.title: Section Workflow
requirements.description: Each section tracks its own status: draft > in-review > approved > locked. Sections can be sent back to draft from review. Show who approved and when.

stories.title: Track section approval workflow
stories.iWant: to advance sections through draft, in-review, approved, and locked statuses
stories.soThat: I can track the approval state of each section in my filing

acceptanceCriteria.then: the section status changes to 'approved' and records who approved and when
the section status changes to 'locked' and content becomes read-only

requirements.title: Dashboard
requirements.description: Project overview showing which sections are blocking, overall completion percentage, team activity, upcoming deadlines.

stories.title: View project dashboard
stories.iWant: to see an overview dashboard with project status, blocking sections, completion percentage, and deadlines
stories.soThat: I can quickly understand where things stand across all my filings

acceptanceCriteria.given: projects exist with sections in various statuses
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a summary card for each project showing completion percentage and deadline

acceptanceCriteria.given: projects exist with sections in various statuses
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see which sections are blocking (in draft or in-review) across all projects

acceptanceCriteria.given: projects exist with team members assigned
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a team activity summary showing recent approvals and status changes
I see upcoming deadlines sorted by urgency
